differential_diagnoses: Severe systemic envenomation (Echis ocellatus) with venom-induced consumption coagulopathy
Placental abruption secondary to venom-induced coagulopathy
Acute kidney injury (likely acute tubular necrosis or acute cortical necrosis)
Compartment syndrome of the lower leg
next_steps: Urgent obstetric consultation for management of potential placental abruption and fetal status
Transfusion of blood products (packed red blood cells, fresh frozen plasma, or cryoprecipitate as indicated by clotting profile)
Transfer to a facility capable of intensive care, renal replacement therapy, and emergency obstetric surgery
Monitoring of urine output and serial assessment of coagulation parameters
